sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if node['color'] in ['N', 'c'] and min(rows) > 0:
        print(f"  color={node['color']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
    if node['color'] == 'G':
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  G cols: [{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check white area
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'W' and min([p[0] for p in node['boundary']]) > 0:
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  W rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Blue at rows 24-27: near the middle white area (rows 24-39)  
- Blue at rows 36-39: also near the middle white area
- Blue at rows 44-47: near the bottom white area (rows 44-63)
And the objects:
- Green gap: rows 40-43, cols 28-29
- Green object: rows 52-57, cols 8-9
- Dark gray object: rows 52-57, cols 10-13
So the green object is in the bottom section (rows 52-57). Maybe the blue button at rows 44-47 controls that section? Let me try clicking that one.

Actually, maybe the goal in Level 2 is different. Perhaps I need to shift multiple sections independently to align things? Let me look at the structure:

- The lower black bar has a green gap

Maybe I need to shift the gray/white boundary in different sections to align the green object with the green gap? The green object is at cols 8-9 and the gap is at cols 28-29. If I can shift the boundary such that the green object moves to cols 28-29...
Actually wait. In Level 1, clicking the blue buttons shifted ALL objects (dark gray and yellow) by 4 columns. In Level 2, clicking the upper blue button shifted the white area boundary but NOT the objects. This is a key difference.
Let me reconsider. Maybe in Level 2, the blue buttons shift the BACKGROUND (white/gray areas), and the objects stay fixed relative to the background. So if I shift the white area rightward, the objects (which are on the gray background) would effectively move leftward relative to the gap?
No wait, that doesn't make sense either because the gap is part of the black bar, which might also shift.
